mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate for future corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns during rate hiking periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive returns occurring in > 60% of co-declining months, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when industrial production falls below its 12-month trend, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and households become sufficiently restrictive to dampen earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 58% of observations where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation expansion outpacing job creation, which suppresses wage growth expectations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive returns dominating (> 55%) during divergence periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows, indicating no short-term pricing of growth data.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of high inflation signals an overheating economy that forces central banks to tighten policy aggressively, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where industrial production accelerates alongside CPI above 195, or the signal loses predictive power in high-inflation regimes.
